title: Why Is It Dark?
lyrics:
[verse 1]
Boom! The light went *splat*—now it's black
My cracker fell—oh no, look back!
The TV’s sleeping, dark and flat
I didn’t say night-night to that

[chorus]
Why is it dark?
Why is it dark?
Tell me why it’s dark

[verse 2]
Did clouds eat up the yellow sun?
Or did the moon take all the fun?
Maybe stars are playing peek
And left me here to count to three

[chorus]
Why is it dark?
Why is it dark?
Tell me why it’s dark

[ending]
I’ll close my eyes and sit real still
The light will come—I know it will
Dark just tucks me in, I see
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, full musical production, xylophone, bass, and tambourine, 130 BPM, super bouncy, high energy, strong groove, clear warm vocal, strong singalong chorus, every word easy to understand
scene: The living room lamp flickers off after a thunderclap, and three-year-old Leo freezes mid-step, his tiny fingers still clutching a half-eaten graham cracker as his wide eyes lock onto the shadow creeping across the carpet where sunlight used to pool. The sudden hush of the refrigerator’s hum makes the darkness feel heavier, and he whispers, *"Why is it dark?"* while staring at the black square of the unlit TV screen.
cover_description: A little boy in pajamas stares at a dark TV, one graham cracker crumb stuck to his cheek.
